# Go Comment Examples

Use Go doc comments immediately before packages, exported types, functions,
methods, constants, and variables. Use ordinary comments for local
implementation notes.

## Package Level

```go
// Package tokens verifies short-lived access tokens issued by the identity
// service.
//
// The package validates signatures and expiry only. Callers remain responsible
// for mapping token subjects to application permissions.
package tokens
```

## File Level

```go
// Code generated by internal/tools/tokenclaims. DO NOT EDIT.
//
// Regenerate with:
//
//     go run ./internal/tools/tokenclaims
//
// The generated field order matches the identity service schema contract.
```

## Type Level

```go
// Verifier validates identity-service tokens for one signing-key set.
//
// A Verifier is safe for concurrent use after construction. It does not refresh
// keys automatically; callers must replace the instance when the key set
// changes.
type Verifier struct {
    // Keys contains the public signing keys trusted by this verifier.
    Keys KeySet

    now func() time.Time
}
```

## Function Level

```go
// Verify parses token and returns its claims when the signature and expiry are
// valid.
//
// Verify returns ErrExpired for otherwise valid tokens whose expiry is before
// the verifier clock. It returns ErrInvalid for malformed tokens, unknown key
// IDs, and signature failures.
func (v Verifier) Verify(token string) (Claims, error) {
    // ...
}
```

## Method Level

```go
// Refresh replaces the verifier key set.
//
// Refresh is safe to call while other goroutines are using the verifier. Calls
// that have already started continue with the previous key set.
func (v *Verifier) Refresh(keys KeySet) {
    // ...
}
```

## Deprecated Identifier

```go
// LegacyVerifier validates tokens signed by the previous identity service.
//
// Deprecated: Use Verifier with a migrated KeySet instead.
type LegacyVerifier struct {
    // ...
}
```

## Implementation Note

```go
// Compare the canonical audience value. Some legacy clients send display names
// in the same field, and accepting them would grant access across applications.
if claims.Audience != expectedAudience {
    return Claims{}, ErrInvalid
}
```
